channel.title: IoT on Jaehyeon Kim

item.title: Why Digital Twins Are Rewiring Industry 4.0
item.description: Beyond CAD Models There is a project by Dassault Systèmes called the Living Heart that illustrates the trajectory of this technology. Instead of relying on standard 2D scans, surgeons can pull up a high-fidelity 3D model of a patient’s heart that simulates blood flow, mechanics, and electricity based on imaging-derived reconstructions and population-based physiological calibration. While the Living Heart is closer to a personalized, highly-parameterized simulation than a continuously streaming IoT system, it highlights the core philosophy of a modern digital twin: moving past static CAD files to create models that are fundamentally aligned with a specific, real-world physical instance.
